quote: Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart and try to love the questions themselves.
through_line: This week’s last question is the oldest one in this brief: should kids still learn to code when machines can do it? Yes. But the answer beneath the answer is Rilke’s: to make anything worth making, you have to bring everything you’ve lived.

articles.title: AI Doesn’t Change Why Kids Should Learn to Code
articles.pitch: The argument that AI makes coding education obsolete gets the logic of literacy exactly wrong. This short but sharp piece makes the case that coding never was mainly about producing code — it was about developing the mental models to understand and shape a world increasingly built on them.

articles.title: Uncoding Creativity in the Age of AI: What Makes a Great Poem, What Makes a Great Storyteller, and What Makes Us Human
articles.pitch: Maria Popova asked ChatGPT for a poem in Whitman’s style. What she received — and why it couldn’t move her — became the starting point for a precise exploration of what makes human creativity irreplaceable: not language, but embodied experience, the transmutation of suffering into meaning.
